title: AutoHeal - User Input Protection: Focusless-First + Focus Theft Recovery
desc: [RULE] Skill content MUST be in English -- Korean uses 2-3x tokens. PHILOSOPHY: focusless-first is the PRIMARY goal. Recovery is safety net only. SELF-IMPROVING: bugs auto-report via suggest, triad dialectic resolves gaps, skill accumulates knowhow. LIVING DOCUMENT: check suggest list periodically.
steps: 0. [RULE] ALL skill steps/desc/tags must be English only -- token efficiency.
1. [PHILOSOPHY] Focusless-first: every CDP action should never need OS foreground. Recovery = safety net. Priority: (1) focusless design, (2) recovery as backstop.
2. [MANDATORY] Restore trigger = end of each ask/CDP action only. Watchdog = monitor+notify only.
3. [MANDATORY] ALL restore paths go through InputReadiness.Probe(SkipBlocker/Knowhow/Zoom=true, AutoApproveYield=true).

4. [FOCUSLESS PATTERN] Preferred: (0) Emulation.setFocusEmulationEnabled(true) -- CDP emulates tab focused WITHOUT OS foreground, no SW minimize needed. Fallback SW pattern: (a) check visibilityState=visible->skip, (b) SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE(8), (c) WaitForWindowStateAsync('minimized', timeout=1000), (d) CDP call, (e) RestoreChromeNoActivate SW(4), (f) WaitForWindowStateAsync('normal', timeout=1000). DEGRADED: if (c) or (f) times out, skip SW step, proceed CDP-only. Ref: SwitchToTargetAsync, BringToFrontAsync.
5. [DEVICE INSERTION = SELF-REPORTING] Insert AutoBugReport at every theft detection point. OnFocusTheft classifies UNGUARDED vs RACE and files suggest automatically.

6. [SELF-IMPROVING LOOP] Bug detected -> suggest filed -> wkappbot ask triad (share skill content + ask for analysis) -> triad dialectic (thesis/antithesis/synthesis) -> code fix -> skill update -> suggest resolve with evidence script. Triad learns from skill history; skill accumulates triad insights. Loop repeats.
7. [OPERATIONAL] Logs: runtime/cdp-focus-risk.jsonl, runtime/cdp-minimize-watchdog.jsonl. Periodic: wkappbot suggest list -> review CDP theft reports -> ask triad if unclear -> fix -> resolve.
8. [INVESTIGATION] When theft: (a) which action, (b) CDP method, (c) IsFocusStealingMethod()?, (d) SW=8?, (e) WaitForWindowState not delay?, (f) OnFocusTheft restores?

9. [DONE] SW(6)->SW(8). BringToFrontAsync WaitForWindowStateAsync. OnFocusTheft ForceForeground+RestoreRetry+AutoBugReport. InputReadiness.Probe. AutoBugReport 5s timeout. EnsureWindowReady ForceForeground. AskGemini prevFg re-capture. DismissDialogAsync consent.
10. [TODO] GPT/Claude ask flows: same prevFgHint+StartFocusWatchdog pattern not yet applied (TODO-10).

11. [TODO -> SOLUTION] Runtime.evaluate JS .focus() bypass (TODO-11): use Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument to monkey-patch HTMLElement.prototype.focus with window.__appbot_lock_focus flag. Set true before CDP input, false after. Also patch window.focus(). window.__appbot_lock_focus toggle via Runtime.evaluate.

12. [GAPS FROM TRIAD v1.16] (A) prevFg must be per-CDP-action scoped, not global -- re-capture before each action in GPT/Claude flows too. (B) WaitForWindowStateAsync timeout -> degraded CDP-only path (implemented in step 4). (C) Emulation.setFocusEmulationEnabled(true) preferred over SW pattern -- added to step 4. (D) Multi-tab concurrent CDP: prevFg collision in triad mode -- needs per-tab/per-run scoping. (E) IsFocusStealingMethod blacklist: Input.dispatchKeyEvent/MouseEvent can steal focus on some Chrome versions -- verify blacklist completeness. (F) Async layout reflow: page may steal focus after RestoreChromeNoActivate if AJAX/render completes post-restore. (G) SPA autofocus/IntersectionObserver: DOM visibility triggers .focus() -- blocked by TODO-11 solution. (H) OS Toast/Permission prompts: CDP window-state management cannot suppress OS-level focus -- accept+log only.
13. [TODO] Implement Emulation.setFocusEmulationEnabled(true/false) in EnsureCdpReadyAsync and BringToFrontAsync as primary focusless mechanism. Test: Chrome 112+.
14. [TODO] Scope prevFg per-CDP-run in GPT/Claude ask flows (Gap-D). Use AsyncLocal<IntPtr> or pass as parameter to avoid triad-mode collision.
author: claude
